title: carex scopulorum var. bracteosa
intro: Carex scopulorum var. bracteosa, also known as Rocky Mountain sedge, is a plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae), characterized by its unique, flat grass-like leaves and distinctive bracteate inflorescence. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Carex scopulorum var. bracteosa include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
